svg.aria-label: Power input and distribution perfboard placement map on a square 2.54 pitch grid
svg.text: Power input and distribution perfboard placement map

One square-pitch through-hole build for the 12 V entry path, LM2596T-5.0-TT buck stage, and the isolated 5 V_A branch. This follows the same board-map model as the CV and gate layouts: on-board part labels, off-board callouts, and a hole table.
Main placement on square pad grid
Top rail is 12 V_IN on row A. Middle rail is 5 V_DIG on row B. Bottom rail is GND on row L. The 5 V_A branch starts after RISO and stays local to the analog loads only.
Wire the high-current loop first, then add the analog branch after 5 V_DIG is stable.
Barrel jack -> F1 -> D1, with C1 and C2 across the input rail
and D2 across 12 V_IN to GND.
Keep U1, L1, D3, C3, and C4 grouped tightly so
Take 5 V_DIG through RISO into a separate 5 V_A node.
Put C5 and C6 only on the analog side of RISO.
Bring the Pi, buck, and analog returns back to the same
L-row ground rail and leave off-board wiring short.
Use heavy wire or solder bridges for the A, B, and L rail runs.
The LM2596T-5.0-TT tab area is large; leave airflow and avoid
C1/C2 are the input decouplers before the buck regulator.
C3/C4 are the digital 5 V output decouplers after the buck loop.
C5/C6 are only for the analog 5 V_A branch used by the reference
If the Pi and motor loads share 5 V_DIG, keep their harness return
separate until it reaches the ground rail.
The TVS D2 is a shunt part, so mount it directly across
the 12 V and GND rails rather than in series.
D1 should be oriented so reverse input power is blocked
This is a practical prototyping map, not a replacement for
a proper high-current PCB if the load grows.
Coordinates below follow the main board above. Off-board parts are the 12 V jack and the two output harnesses. Everything else mounts on the perfboard grid.
Panel or chassis mounted. Bring short leads to the board and keep polarity obvious.
Place directly after the jack before the reverse-polarity diode.
Band faces the regulator side so normal current flows from jack to VIN.
Keep these close to the VIN entry so surges and ripple are handled before the buck loop.
Mount upright with room around the tab. Keep L1, D3, C3, and C4 close to it.
These parts define the switching-output loop. Do not spread them apart on the board.
All analog reference and CV-output loads should leave from this node, not from raw 5 V_DIG.
Run separate harnesses for digital and analog consumers so the analog branch stays quiet.
